name: Rhubarb Sorbet
recipeInstructions:
Prepare and cook the rhubarb at least a day ahead of serving the sorbet. Wipe and trim the fruit, then slice it thinly into a flameproof casserole. Sprinkle the finely grated orange zest over it and drizzle with 4 tablespoons of honey. Cover and leave in a cool place for 2 hours or more until the sweetener has drawn out some of the rhubarb juices; this liquid will prevent sticking during cooking.
Cook the rhubarb in the covered dish until perfectly tender – I think this is best done in a low oven, but you can use a very gentle flame on top of the stove if preferred. Let the cooked rhubarb cool slightly, then stir in the remaining 4 tablespoons honey and blend to a smooth puree using a food processor.
When the puree is quite cold, freeze it in a chilled loaf tin or other suitable container until firm around the edges. Beat the half-frozen sorbet until slushy. Whisk the egg whites and fold them in so the mixture looks like pale pink snow. Cover and freeze until solid. Beat to break up ice crystals and increase bulk, then freeze again until ready to serve. Shortbread fingers make a good crunchy accompaniment.
